# Contract: IOB Reader FFI (UniFFI Swift boundary)

**Feature**: `011-iob-cc-reader` | **Date**: 2026-07-17
**Module**: `kaname-core` (Rust) → `KanameCore` (Swift, generated by UniFFI)

Additive to the P1 + ICICI + HDFC + SBI + Yes + Federal `engine-ffi` contracts. The "external interface"
is the exported UniFFI surface (not an HTTP API); generated Swift signatures are the consumer-facing
form. **Reuses** the existing `Decimal`/`NaiveDate` custom types, the `Direction` enum, and the
`ParsedStatement`/`ParsedTransaction` records **unchanged** — IOB adds **two functions and no new type**.
IOB is a credit-card line reader, so — like SBI/Yes/Federal and **unlike** the bank-account readers —
its parse export takes **no `first_row_words` parameter**.

---

## Exported functions

### `read_iob_statement`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn read_iob_statement(lines: Vec<String>, full_text: String) -> ParsedStatement` |
| Swift | `public func readIobStatement(lines: [String], fullText: String) -> ParsedStatement` |

**Contract**
- Parses **already-extracted** text `lines` (one statement row per element) + `full_text` (used only
  for enrichment). **Never** opens a file or PDF (FR-017).
- **Single layout**: `DD-MON-YYYY <merchant> <amount> Dr|Cr` — a two-digit day, an **uppercase**
  three-letter month, a four-digit year, description, amount, and a terminal two-letter `Dr`/`Cr`
  marker. Produces one `ParsedTransaction` per matching row; non-matching lines (header, `Credit Card
  Number` line, `ACCOUNT SUMMARY` block, the summary values row, `Total Purchase`, the end-of-statement
  marker) are ignored (FR-005); rows matching the shape but failing date/amount parse go to
  `ParsedStatement.errored_lines` (FR-016) — **never throws, never aborts**. A statement with no
  matching rows returns empty `lines` with no error.
- **Uppercase month**: `31-MAR-2026 → 2026-03-31`, `04-APR-2026 → 2026-04-04` via the existing
  case-insensitive `%d-%b-%Y` (no IOB-specific date code) (FR-003, D4).
- **Direction from the terminal `Dr`/`Cr` marker**: `Cr → .credit`, `Dr → .debit` (via
  `classify(desc, dir, None)`) — never the amount's sign/magnitude or the description's wording; the
  marker wins even when the description contains a credit/debit word, and a small `Cr` (1000.00) is a
  credit while a larger `Dr` (3500.00) is a debit (FR-008/009, SC-005).
- **Amount** is an exact non-negative `Decimal` with scale preserved; Indian thousands grouping is
  stripped (FR-006/007, SC-006). Money never crosses as `f64`.
- **Enriches** `period_end` (from the lone `Stmt Date : <DD-MON-YYYY>`, case-insensitive, spacing
  tolerant) and `card_last4` (masked PAN via the `Credit Card Number` anchor, whole-text fallback) from
  `full_text` when present. **`period_start` is always `nil`** — IOB prints no period range and MUST NOT
  fabricate one (FR-010). `123456XXXXXX0042 16000 25091.5` → `cardLast4 == "0042"` (**not** `6000`/`5091`
  from the adjacent limits); no recognizable PAN → `nil` (never fabricated) (FR-011/012, D7).
- **Reconciliation carve-out (FR-013, SC-013)**: the result carries **only** rows + `period_end` +
  `card_last4` (+ errored lines). It exposes **no** printed debit/credit totals, even when `full_text`
  contains the `ACCOUNT SUMMARY` block with `Payment / Credits` / `Purchases / Debits` figures — those
  are ignored.
- **Pure & deterministic**: identical input ⇒ identical output; no network, clock, locale, or global
  state (FR-018/021). Total function — no failure surfaced to Swift.

**Consumer expectations (app/test)**
- Call with PDFKit-extracted lines + full text. Treat `errored_lines` as review items; still consume
  every `lines` entry. Do not infer direction from `amount` — use `direction`. Treat `cardLast4 == nil`
  or `periodStart == nil` as "unknown", never as a value to fabricate (IOB always leaves `periodStart`
  nil). Do not expect printed totals (they are not in the model this slice).

### `iob_claims`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn iob_claims(full_text: String) -> bool` |
| Swift | `public func iobClaims(fullText: String) -> Bool` |

**Contract**
- Returns `true` iff `full_text` contains an IOB issuer marker (`"INDIAN OVERSEAS BANK"` **or**
  `"iobnet.co.in"`, case-insensitive) — the document-plausibility gate (FR-001).
- MUST return `false` for other issuers' statements (e.g. an ICICI, HDFC, or SBI document) so a
  statement is never misattributed to IOB (FR-002, US1-AC4). Pure, deterministic, no I/O.

> Internally: `read_iob_statement` wraps `read_lines(&IobReader, …)` (single layout — **not** the HDFC
> composite); `iob_claims` delegates to `claims(&IobReader, …, "IOB")`. `lib.rs` re-exports both
> functions (as it does the SBI/Yes/Federal pairs); `statement/mod.rs` gains `pub mod iob;`.

---

## Types (all reused — no new type)

- `record ParsedStatement` / `record ParsedTransaction` — **unchanged** from the landed slices. IOB
  populates `periodEnd` and `cardLast4 == "0042"`, leaves `periodStart == nil`, and (as a credit-card
  reader) leaves `ledger == nil` per row and `printedOpeningBalance`/`printedClosingBalance == nil`.
  **No `printed_total_*` fields exist** (reconciliation carve-out).
- `enum Direction { Debit, Credit }` → Swift `.debit` / `.credit` (P1, `uniffi::Enum`).
- Custom types `Decimal`↔`String`↔`Foundation.Decimal` and `NaiveDate`↔ISO-8601 `String`
  (P1, in `ffi.rs` + `uniffi.toml`) — **unchanged**. Money never crosses as `f64`.

> Swift shape (for reference, unchanged): `ParsedStatement { bankCode: String; lines:
> [ParsedTransaction]; erroredLines: [String]; periodStart: String?; periodEnd: String?; cardLast4:
> String?; printedOpeningBalance: String?; printedClosingBalance: String?; confidence: Double }`.

---

## Contract tests (test-first — FR-024/026)

**Rust (`cargo test`)** — engine-side truth (`tests/parity.rs` + `iob.rs` unit tests):
- **Golden parity**: `read_iob_statement(basic.lines, basic.full_text)` equals the fixture's `expected`
  exactly — rows `2026-03-31 / 1000.00 / Credit / INR / "ExampleRefundMerchant"` and
  `2026-04-04 / 3500.00 / Debit / INR / "ExampleStorePurchase"`; `period_start` **absent (None)**;
  `period_end 2026-04-20`; **`card_last4 "0042"`**; `errored_lines` empty (SC-001/003/011).
- **Determinism**: two calls on the same input are equal.
- **Wrong issuer**: `iob_claims("ICICI Bank Statement") == false`; `iob_claims(basic.full_text) == true`
  (FR-002, SC-002) — the `iob_claims_accepts_own_document_and_rejects_others` test.
- **Direction from marker**: a `Cr` row is Credit and a `Dr` row is Debit regardless of magnitude (the
  1000.00 `Cr` is credit, the larger 3500.00 `Dr` is debit); a row whose description contains a
  credit/debit word but whose terminal marker conflicts classifies from the **marker** (SC-005).
- **Reconciliation carve-out**: the `full_text` contains the `ACCOUNT SUMMARY` printed totals, yet the
  result yields **only** rows + `period_end` + last-4 (SC-013).
- **`period_start` absent**: the result's `period_start` is `None` (IOB prints no range) (SC-003).
- **Malformed row**: a line matching the IOB shape but with an unparseable date lands in
  `errored_lines`, valid rows still returned, no panic (reuses the existing seam behaviour).

**Swift (Swift Testing, `import KanameCore`)** — "core ↔ Swift IOB parse":
- `readIobStatement(...)` over the two golden lines → 2 `lines`; `lines[0]` = `2026-03-31`,
  `Decimal(string:"1000.00")`, `.credit`, `"INR"`, `"ExampleRefundMerchant"`; `lines[1]` = `2026-04-04`,
  `Decimal(string:"3500.00")`, `.debit`, `"ExampleStorePurchase"`.
- `periodStart == nil`; `periodEnd == "2026-04-20"`; **`cardLast4 == "0042"`**.
- `iobClaims(fullText:) == true` for the IOB text and `false` for an ICICI/SBI string.
- Amounts compared as `Foundation.Decimal` value-equality (exact; never float).

---

## Stability / compatibility
- Adding two functions is additive; reusing the records means **no wire-format change** and no new
  Swift type.
- The generated `Generated/kaname_core.swift` + `KanameCoreFFI.xcframework` are build artifacts, rebuilt
  by `make core-xcframework` (which runs **before** `tuist generate`). **This contract**, not the
  generated files, is the reviewed source of truth.
